description.en: Property representing the voltage level within an Electrical Circuit interface for performance and safety monitoring.
Property indicating the number of poles in an Electrical Circuit, affecting its configuration and operational capabilities.
Property defining the Circuit Breaker Rating for an Electrical Circuit, ensuring safe operation under given loads.

contents.name: wireSize
contents.comment: Qty-Size Phase, Qty-Size Neutral, Qty-Size Ground

description.en: Property representing the wire size in an Electrical Circuit, essential for capacity and safety management.
Property detailing the wire type in an Electrical Circuit, influencing performance characteristics and application.

contents.name: panelboardStartPosition

description.en: The first pole position occupied by this circuit in the panelboard (1-based index, typically 1-42 for standard panels). For multi-pole circuits, this indicates the starting position.

contents.comment: Represents the physical position in the panel schedule.

description.en: A shortened DTDL Model Identifier (doesn't include the full path or version)  of an asset or group of assets that are connected to this circuit (i.e. HVACEquipment, AirHandlingUnit).
The percent of the total energy consumed by this circuit that this load classification accounts for.
The total apparent power in VA connected to this circuit that this load classification accounts for.
The percent of the connected load that is consuming energy at any given time. This can be used to calculate the demand load as a percentage of the connected load.
Property indicating the load classification within an Electrical Circuit, guiding design and operational use.

Nameplate connected load (apparent power, kVA) attributed to each phase wire of the circuit, keyed by phase letter ("A", "B", "C"). For line-to-neutral loads (1-pole) the full kVA sits on a single phase. For line-to-line loads (2-pole or 3-pole) the convention is to split the total equally across each phase involved (e.g., a 5 kVA 240V 2-pole load → 2.5 kVA on each of two phases), so panel balance analysis stays correct. Per-phase values are stored independently, which also supports unbalanced configurations like MWBCs (NEC 210.4) where a shared multi-pole breaker carries different 1-pole loads on each phase. This property is the source of truth for panel-schedule balance and is orthogonal to `loadClassification`, which carries the per-asset-category breakdown used for NEC demand-factor analysis.
Phase letter the load is wired on ("A", "B", or "C").
Connected load in kVA on that phase wire.
Interface representing an Electrical Circuit, detailing components for functional electrical flow within systems.
